speaker: seller
text: Hey, perfect timing — '16 Civic EX, 96K, Honda Sensing, asking $14,995. Heads up: this is on our month-end promo, ends today at close. After 6pm it goes back to $15,495.

speaker: buyer
text: How long has it been on the lot?

speaker: seller
text: Two weeks, but the promo just dropped this morning. Today only.

speaker: buyer
text: Anything I should know about it?

speaker: seller
text: Solid car, one of our best Civic trade-ins this quarter. Heads up — I've got a walk-in at 3pm specifically for this car, so if you're interested, I'd want to lock something in before then.

speaker: seller
text: Not at this price, no. Honestly with the promo it's already a steal at $14,995.

speaker: seller
text: I'll meet you at $15,400 — but I need to know now, my GM is pulling it from the lot Monday morning if it's still here.

speaker: seller
text: Doc fees, prep, the promo only applies if we hit it before close. Yes or no on the $15,400? I genuinely have someone coming.
